nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联174种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等174种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: Warialda
nodes.summary: Warialda是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括快速鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 阿德莱德
nodes.summary: 阿德莱德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括快速鸚鵡、Spotted scrubwren、Noisy miner，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Griffith, New South Wales
nodes.summary: Griffith, New South Wales是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括快速鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 塔斯馬尼亞州
nodes.summary: 塔斯馬尼亞州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联47种鸟类，包括澳大利亚麻鸭、Brown quail、Hoary-headed grebe等47种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新南威爾士州
nodes.summary: 新南威爾士州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、叢塚雉、Black-eared cuckoo等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 快速鸚鵡
nodes.summary: 快速鸚鵡（Swift parrot，学名 Lathamus discolor），在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，已关联6个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括森林砍伐、栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: 森林
nodes.summary: 森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联402种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan等402种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

links.evidence: thumb|300px|Mudgereeba, SE Queensland, Australia Usually inhabiting: forests, woodlands, agricultural land and plantations, and also in urban areas

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: |last3=Stojanovic |first3=Dejan |date=2024 |title=Influence of molecular marker type on estimating effective population size and other genetic parameters in a critically endangered parrot |journal=Ecology and Evolution |language=en |volume=14 |issue=3 |article-number=e11102 |doi=10.1002/ece3.11102 |pmid=38524913 |issn=2045-7758 |pmc=10961163|bibcode=2024EcoEv..1411102O }} This supports the results of earlier studies that use demographic information about swift parrots to predict that the species could be extinct by 2031. Habitat for the critically endangered swift parrot is being "knowingly destroyed" by logging because of government failures to manage the species' survival.{{Cite journal |last1=Webb |first1=Matthew H

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Birds Australia, Melbourne Logging has already had severe impacts on habitat availability in recent decades and there is evidence that up to 23% of swift parrot breeding habitat has been logged just in the Southern Forests region of Tasmania over the last 20 years. Deforestation also affects the rate of predation by sugar gliders – where mature forest cover is diminished, parrots suffer worse predation rates. Sugar glider predation is worst where logging is severe; these threats interact in a synergistic manner. Given the severity of deforestation across the breeding range, and the relationship between deforestation and sugar glider predation intensity, habitat loss in critical breeding areas of Tasmania may be the species most severe threat
